def: n. m. End (انت 3). Maāl-i-kār kā apne nahīṅ khayāl ātā, Milāyā karte haiṅ maṭṭī meṅ gor-kan maṭṭī . Ātish. His own end he ne'er thinks about, from day to day The gravedigger joins mortal clay with other clay. maāl-andesh , n. m. One who looks to the issues of things. maāl-andeshī , n. f. Consideration of the end or ultimate results. khair-maāl , n. m. A happy issue.
more: (from اول) s. m. End, aim, consequence, termination, issue, tendency. خير مآل ḵẖair maʼāl, A happy issue or tendency. مآل انديشي maʼāl-andesh-ī, s. f. Consideration of the end or consequence.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَآل (maāl), borrowed from Arabic مَآل (maʔāl).

def: formal plural of مَآل (maāl)
s.m. pl. (of معلاة maʻlāt), Highnesses, eminences.

ety: acc. of maʻa
def: adv. Along with, together, at the same time; — at once; instantly.
more: adv. Together, at the same time, all at once.

def: (for A. معانٍ maʻāniṅ, pl. of معنی maʻnā; see maʻnī), s.f. pl. Meanings, intended significations or senses; imports; — mental images; — intrinsic qualities; — qualities that are commended or approved, commendable qualities, charms, graces; elegance of language; — elegant mystical meanings; sublime realities; spiritual matters; — (for ʻilmuʼl-maʻānī), the science whereby is known the manner of adapting language to the requirements of the case; — ability (or knowledge how) to express clearly one meaning in various ways.
more: s. plur. (of معني) Meanings; elegant or mystical meanings, elegance of language.

def: s.f. pl. (of maʼrabat, v.n. fr. أرب 'to be in want'), Necessary things, necessities, wants, desires.
more: (from ارب) s. m. The time or place of any necessary business. ¶ maʼārib, s. plur. (of ماربة) Necessary things, necessity.

def: s.m. pl. (of maʻrifa, or maʻrifat, q.v.), Sorts of knowledge; sciences; acquirements.

ety: inf. n., and n. of place, fr. عاش (for عيش
def: s.f. Living, life; — that whereby one lives, means of living, livelihood, subsistence; landed property; — the place in which one lives
more: n. f. عيش 1. Livelihood ( ājīvkā , 1). 2. Landed property. maāsh-dār , The owner of an estate. tarz-i-maāsh, tarīqa-i-maāsherat , Way of life; social condition. maāsh'rat , n. f. Social life; society. nek-maāsh . One who earns an honest livelihood.
(noun of place of عيش) s. f. Means or place of living, life, living, livelihood, subsistence.
subs: adj. & s.m. Possessed of (or one who is possessed of) the means of living or of a maintenance; — owner of an estate
vulg. bad-māsh, adj. & s.m. Wicked, bad; — one who leads a bad life
s.f. Style of living, way or manner of life; social condition
adj. & s.m. Living a good or an honest life; — one who earns an honest livelihood.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَعَاش (ma'āš), borrowed from Arabic مَعَاش (maʕāš), from عَاشَ (ʕāša).

ety: pass. part. of عشّر 'to make ten'
def: adj. & s.m. Relating to ten; composed of ten; ten-sided; — a ten-sided figure, a decagon.
(for A. معاصٍ maʻāṣiṉ, pl. of maʻṣiyat, q.v.), s.f. pl. Acts of disobedience, sins, crimes.
more: s. plur. (of معصية) Sins. ¶ muʼāﻌṣī (part. act. III. of عصي) adj. Rebellious.

def: s.m. pl. (of مأثرة maʼs̤arat, inf. n. of اثر), Generous qualities or actions (related, or handed down by tradition, from ancestors), worthy or illustrious acts; causes of glorying; glories, excellencies; — traditions, histories; signs, memorials (used as last member of compounds, e.g. ẕafar-maʼās̤ir, 'glorying in victory, victorious').
more: s. m. plur. (of ماثرة mās̤arat, from اثر) Signs, marks, memorials, worthy actions.

def: (pl. of maʻāb, or maʻābat, 'a ground or cause for finding fault, a vice,' &c., n. of place fr. عاب (for عيب); or an anomalous pl. of ʻaib, q.v.), s.m. pl. Vices, faults, defects, blemishes.
(for aʻūẕu maʻāẕaʼl-lāh), I seek preservation, or protection, by God; Heaven defend me; God forbid (maʻāẕa is the inf. n., in the acc. case, of عاذ for عوذ 'to seek preservation or protection').
more: God be the refuge! God forbid!

ety: n. of place fr. عبد 'to worship'
def: s.m. A place of worship, a mosque, a temple, a church; — a place of pilgrimage.
more: n. m. عود 1. place of worship; mosque. 2. A place of pilgrimage.
(noun of place from عبد Heb. Chald. and Aram. ) s. m. A place of worship, temple, mosque, church. ¶ muʼaﻌbbid (part. act. II.) s. m. An adorer.
ex: بھگوان ہوتا تو اس معبد کے اندر ہوتا، دہلیز پر نہ بیٹھا کرتا۔
If there was a god, he would have been inside this temple, not lingering at the entrance.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مَعْبَد (maʕbad).

ety: ma, 'not'+bād, optat. of būdan, 'to be'
def: adv. May (or let) it not be, be it not; by no means, away! God forbid; lest; perhaps, perchance.
more: (from بودن) Be it not; by no means, God forbid! lest.

ety: ma, 'not'+bād, optat. of būdan, 'to be'
def: adv. May (or let) it not be, be it not; by no means, away! God forbid; lest; perhaps, perchance.
more: Be it not; by no means; God forbid; lest.
(from بودن) Be it not; by no means, God forbid! lest.

def: s.f. pl. (of mabdā, q.v.), Beginnings, origins; elements
more: s. f. plur. (of مبدأ from بدأ) Beginnings, origins.

def: see s.v. mā. s.m. (in Prosody) A molossus or metrical foot of three long syllables; — a magical or mystical formula; a name of Brahmā, of Vishṇu, and of Śiva.
s.m. pl. (of mablag, q.v.), Sums (of money), moneys.

ety: n. of place fr. عبر 'to cross'
def: s.m. A place where a river is crossed, a ferry; a ford; — a pass.
more: (part. act. II. of عبر Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. An interpreter of dreams. ¶ maﻌbar (noun of place) s. m. A pass, ferry, ford. ¶ miﻌbar (noun of instrument) A ferry-boat.

def: s.f. pl. (of mabarrat, inf. n. of برّ 'to be pious,' &c.), Pious acts, good works; public buildings or institutions (as inns, &c.), built by pious Muslims.

ety: n. of place and time fr. بدأ 'to begin.' &c.
def: s.m. Beginning, original, origin, source; principle, or first rule (of a science, &c.).
more: (noun of place from بدأ) s. m. The beginning, origin, principle, source.

ety: n. of place fr. بدع 'to produce, to originate'
def: s.m. The place where anything new is produced; — first production, invention, discovery; fiction.
more: n. m. 1. Source; origin. 2. The first cause; Creator.
(part. act. IV. of بدع) s. m. Inventor, producer, creator.

ety: pass. part. of بغض 'to be or become hateful or odious'
def: part. Hated, detested; odious; accounted an enemy.
more: (part. pass. of بغض) adj. Detested, hated, accounted an enemy.

ety: n. of place, fr. بحث 'to search or inquire' (into
def: s.m. Time. or place, for inquiry or investigation, or for disputation; — dissertation, investigation, disputation, discussion, debate.
more: (from بحث) s. m. Time or place for disputation; disputation, investigation.

ety: pass. part. of بہت 'to be or become confounded or perplexed,' &c.
def: part. Confounded, perplexed, astonished, amazed; dazed, stupefied; struck dumb, mute with astonishment, dismayed
subs: adj. Somewhat confounded, &c.; thoroughly astonished, quite struck.

def: (part. pass. of بـﮩت Chald. ) adj. Stupified, astonished, struck dumb with astonishment, confounded, amazed.

ety: inf. n., and n. of place, of بيت 'to pass the night,' &c.
def: s.f. Passing the night; — a place in which one passes the night; a place of rest at night, bed-chamber.
more: (infin. or noun of place from بيت) s. Passing the night, place of repose at night, a bedchamber.

ety: n. of place fr. بلغ 'to reach,' &c.
def: s.m. Place of arrival; utmost point or scope or degree (of an attainment, &c.), utmost degree of proficiency; perfection; — sum (resulting from addition); amount or product (resulting from multiplication); quantity; produce; — a sum (of money), ready money; — adj. Much, many.
more: n. m. A sum of money.
(noun of place from بلغ) s. m. 1. The place of arrival. 2. (in Pers.) A sum, ready money. 3. Much, many.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُبَلِّغ (muballiġ, “missionary”), itself from Arabic مُبَلِّغ (muballiḡ).

ety: n. of place fr. بني 'to build'
def: s.m. A foundation.
more: (noun of place from بنا) s. m. A foundation. مبني فساد mabnī-i-fasād, An aggressor.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مَبْنًى (mabnan).

ety: pass. part. of بنی 'to build,' &c.
def: part. Built, constructed, formed; founded, based (on); — (in Gram.) indeclinable (noun); — s.m. An edifice, a fabric.
more: adj. Founded; based. mabnī h. , v. n. To be based upon.
(noun of place from بنا) s. m. A foundation. مبني فساد mabnī-i-fasād, An aggressor.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مَبْنًى (mabnan).

ety: pass. part. of بسط 'to spread, expand,' &c.
def: part. Spread out, extended, expanded, dilated, stretched out, outstretched.

ety: pass. part. of عبد 'to worship'
def: part. Worshipped, adored; — s.m. What is worshipped or adored, a god, deity; — the Deity.
more: n. m. عبد lit . what is adored. God; deity.
(part. pass. of عبد) adj. Adored; (subst.) What is adored, god, deity.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian معبود (ma'būd), from Arabic مَعْبُود (maʕbūd).

ety: perf. part. of أبن; lit. 'made an object of imputation, suspected of evil'
def: adj. & s.m. Suspected of evil; infamous; — a catamite; a wretch held in abhorrence.

ety: pass. part. of بذل 'to give liberally,' &c.
def: part. Expended, bestowed; lavished; — s.m. A magnificent present.
more: (part. pass. of بذل) adj. Expended, bestowed.

ety: prob. S. मृषा, rt. मृष्; or fr. S. मच् or मञ्च्
def: s.f. (dialec.) Forgetfulness, negligenee, inattention, apathy, drowsiness.
s.f. (dialec.) A model or sample for judging of the purity of gold.

ety: Prk. मंचअं or मच्चअं (?); S. मञ्चकं
def: s.m. A platform, a frame or stage (on which a watchman sits to drive away birds, &c. from corn-fields); — a large bedstead, a bed
more: n. m. S. मञ्च A large bedstead used by Zamindars. māchā-toṛ , n. m. 1. A bed-presser (Shakes.); a lazy person who never leaves his bed ( palaṅg-toṛ , 1). 2. A Rājpūt soldier very indolent and much addicted to opium, but active and brave when roused.

s. m. 1. A large bedstead or platform. 2. A frame, or stage on which they sit to drive away birds, &c. from corn-fields. ماچا توڙ māchā-toṛ, adj. 1. A lazy person who never stirs from his bed. 2. A kind of soldier among the Rājpūts, very indolent and much addicted to opium, but active and brave when roused.
subs: s.m. 'Bed-breaker'; one who sticks to his bed, a lazy person who rarely stirs from his bed; — a kind of soldier among the Rājpūts (very indolent and much addicted to opium, but active and brave when roused)
adj. & s.m. Stupid, foolish; — a stupid or foolish fellow.

def: s.m. = māćā, q.v.
subs: adj. & s.m. See s.v. māćā. s.m. A platform, a frame or stage (on which a watchman sits to drive away birds, &c. from corn-fields); — a large bedstead, a bed

s.m. 'Bed-breaker'; one who sticks to his bed, a lazy person who rarely stirs from his bed; — a kind of soldier among the Rājpūts (very indolent and much addicted to opium, but active and brave when roused)
adj. & s.m. Stupid, foolish; — a stupid or foolish fellow.

ety: dialec. or prov.
def: s.m. = maćān, q.v. s.m. A platform; scaffold; stage; raised seat; bench; a large bedstead.
more: n. m. A large lump of flesh or meat. Gosht kā muchchā mūṅh meṅ de dūṅgā! (Said to a Hindu).
s. m. A large lump of flesh or meat. (See H. موچا mochā ).

ety: see maćaknā
def: s.f. Creak or pain in the joints.
more: (from مچکنا) s. f. Pain in the joints.

ety: maćak˚ = Prk. मच्चक्क(इ), or मच्चक्के(इ), fr. S. मच् (pass. मच्य˚) + कृ
def: v.n. To creak (as a bedstead, &c.); — to have pains in the joints.
more: v. n. 1. To have pain in the joints. 2. To creak (as, a bedstead, &c.).

ety: Prk. मच्छलो, and मच्छलओ; S. मत्सरः, and मत्सर + कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Wayward, headstrong, obstinate, perverse; refractory, disobedient; restive, fidgety; pert; cross; squeamish, fastidious.
s.m. (dialec.) = maćān, q.v. s.m. A platform; scaffold; stage; raised seat; bench; a large bedstead.

ety: maćal, q.v.+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To be wayward, or perverse, or obstinate; to insist (upon); to be refractory, or disobedient; to be cross; — to sulk, pout, &c. (as a wayward child).
more: v. n. 1. To be wayward, perverse, obstinate; to insist upon. 2. To sulk; pout; cry; roll on the ground in a pet (children). Are! oh machle pāchhe nāṅh ṭale . Rus. That stubborn thing will never give in.
v. n. To be perverse, refractory, disobedient, cross, obstinate.

def: s.m. = maćlā-pan, and maćlāʼī, qq.v. s.m. Waywardness, &c. ( = maćlāʼī, q.v.) s.f. Waywardness, perverseness, obstinacy, stubbornness; pertness; crossness; fidgetiness; squeamishness.

ety: by redupl. fr. S. मच् or मंच्
def: adj. Filled up, stuffed full, crammed, choke full; close, thick; — s.m. Creaking ( = mać-mać).
more: n. m. Creaking (of a bed, etc.).

ety: S. मञ्चक + लः, Prk. अल्लो or अलो
def: s.m. A platform; scaffold; stage; raised seat; bench; a large bedstead.
more: n. m. S. मञ्च 1. Aloft ( ṭāṅḍ ). 2. See jauṅḍā .
s. m. A platform, scaffold, stage, raised seat. See منچ.

ety: caus. of maćnā; and = mać˚+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To raise up, stir up, set up, excite; — to cause, make, produce; to commit, perpetrate
more: v. a. Caus. of مچنا q. v. 1. To make; cause; commit; perpetrate. 2. To stir up; excite. Āge mil gaë naṅd ke ḍhoṭā, de de kīk machāë dai hāṅsī, Ghūṅgaṭ ke paṭ khul gaë, doëj te ho gaī pūranmāshī . Song. Nand's son I met, he shrieked with laughter at our interview, My parted veil flew up, the new moon into full moon grew. machā'ū , ( In comp .) One who causes or excites.
(caus. of مچنا) v. a. To make, to cause, to stir up, excite, commit, perpetrate, to produce.

ety: maćā(nā), q.v. + ū = Prk. इउओ = S. (इ)तृ+कः
def: s.m. One who raises or stirs up, exciter, &c. (used as last member of compounds).
v.t. = maćānā, q.v. v.t. To raise up, stir up, set up, excite; — to cause, make, produce; to commit, perpetrate

ety: Prk. मच्छो; S.मत्स्यः
def: s.m. (f. -ī), A fish; — name of the first of the ten incarnations or manifestations of Vishṇu (see matsya).

ety: Prk. मच्छडो; S. मक्ष + रः
def: s.m. A mosquito; a large gnat; a midge; — maććhar-kī jhūl, s.f. 'The covering or coat of a gnat,' anything minute or insignificant
more: n. m. S. मशक; Pālī, makaso . A musquito; a large gnat.; midge. 1. Māchhar kat-khān kareṅ rātoṅ, Ṭol bāṅdh-kar phaujan ḍhālāṅ, Mo par āë chaṛheṅ rātoṅ . Rus. Wom. Mosquitoes bite me all the night, Shoals on shoals on me alight, In force they come on me all night. 2. Māchhar ne kāyā khāī rī, ṭhaur dank mār deh māṅ, kūpe ḍhāl sujāī rī . Rus. Wom. Mosquitoes have my body eaten up quite, All over I am stung by their sharp bite, I'm swollen like an oil-can to the sight. machchhar kī jhūl , n. f. lit . a musquito's coat. Any thing insignificant. machchhar kī jhūl kā chor. lit . one who'd steal a coat off a gnat. A petty pilferer.
s. m. A musquito, a gnat. مچهر کي جهول کا چور machchhar kī jhūl kā chor, One who would steal the minutest thing, a petty pilferer.
subs: One who would steal the minutest thing, a petty pilferer.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀫𑀘𑁆𑀙𑀭 (macchara), from Sanskrit मत्सर (matsara). Compare Gujarati મચ્છર (macchar).

def: s.f. A kiss, a buss (cf. Pers. māć)
subs: (-kī), To take a kiss (from), to kiss.

ety: Prk. मच्छिआ; S. मत्स्य + इका
def: s.f. A fish ( = maćhlī, q.v.). s.f. A fish; — the sign Pisces; — the fish-insect; — an ear-ring made in the form of a fish; — a muscle or tendon (of the arm or leg, — e.g. tumhārī to ḵẖūb maćhliyāṅ khaṛī-huʼī haiṅ)
more: n. f. 1. See machhlī . 2. A buss; a kiss (to a child). 1. Is kitābī muṅh kī ek machchhī, e dugānā jān! do! Maiṅ nahā dho-kar hūṅ āī, chūmne Qurān do! 2. Lab woh aise ki jān de dīje, Dahan aisā ki machchhiyāṅ līje . Shauq. Such lips for which your life you'd give, A mouth to kiss which you would live. machchhī lenā , v. n. To kiss; give a kiss.
s. f. (dakh. s. m.) A fish.
s. f. (dakh.) Fish: see مچهـ machh.
subs: (-meṅ), Muscle to form or be developed (in)
To flounder like a fish (out of water); to be uneasy or restless; to jump about in agony
s.m. (f. -wālī), One who sells fish, fishmonger

ety: Prk. मच्छो; S. मत्स्यः
def: s.m. (f. -ī), A fish (cf. maćh).
s.m. corr. of maksh, q.v. s.m. Hypocrisy; — wrath; — multitude.
n. m. A pile engine.
A stupid foolish fellow.
s.m. = maććhar, q.v. s.m. A mosquito; a large gnat; a midge; — maććhar-kī jhūl, s.f. 'The covering or coat of a gnat,' anything minute or insignificant
subs: One who would steal the minutest thing, a petty pilferer.

def: (from مچانا) s. m. (dakh.) in compos. Maker, causer.

n. S. मत्स्य; Pālī, machcho . A large fish. The fifth avatār or incarnation, when Vishnu appeared in the shape of the small fish supharī to Satyavrata or Hayagrivā to warn him of the general deluge and request him to place the four Veds in the boat which he ( Vishnu ) would preserve.
more: s. m. 1. A fish in general. 2. The name of the first avatār, or incarnation, when Vish̤ṇu appeared in the shape of the small fish ( शफरी ) sapharī, to Satyavrata, or Hayagrīva, to warn him of the general deluge, and desire him to place the four Vedas in the boat which he ( Vish̤ṇu ) would preserve. The fish was first taken up by Hayagrīva in his hands when bathing, being then of the size of the sapharī (or ponṭhī ); but, it grew too large for being contained in a tank, river, &c. and was carried to the sea; where, afterwards, it supported the ark with its horn. Satyavrata lived about 3000 years before Christ.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀫𑀘𑁆𑀙 (maccha), from Sanskrit मत्स्य (mátsya).

def: s. m. (dakh.) A fisherman.
v. n. (dakh.) See مچنا machnā.

ety: Prk. मच्छिआ = S. मक्षिका
def: s.f. A fly ( = makkhī, q.v.). s.f. A fly; — the sight (of a gun)
subs: (or makkhiyāṅ) uṛānā or jhalnā (-par-se), To drive away flies (from off); — to perform servile offices (for); to flatter, to fawn (upon); — to have ulcers on the body (so as to be constantly employed in driving away the flies from them); — to fritter away the time, to trifle, to idle
(-par-se), To knock off a fly (from a thing or place, with a ball or an arrow), to be a good marksman
s.m. lit. 'One who would suck a fly rather than lose any of the food or drink into which it fell'; a miser, niggard, skinflint
s.m. One who kills a fly or flies; — a dirty, fllthy fellow; an idler, a lazy fellow; — one who makes an exact copy of a document, &c., mistakes, and blots, &c. included
To kill flies; to be unemployed, to idle, to trifle, to fool (syn. makkhī uṛānā)
(-par), Flies to buzz (about), or to sit (upon); — to be disgusting, or ugly, &c.
To swallow a living fly; — to bring evil upon oneself; — to tell great lies
Not to allow a fly to sit on the nose; — to shrink from incurring an obligation.

def: s.f. = maććhī, q.v. s.f. A fish; — the sign Pisces; — the fish-insect; — an ear-ring made in the form of a fish; — a muscle or tendon (of the arm or leg, — e.g. tumhārī to ḵẖūb maćhliyāṅ khaṛī-huʼī haiṅ)
subs: (-meṅ), Muscle to form or be developed (in)
To flounder like a fish (out of water); to be uneasy or restless; to jump about in agony
s.m. (f. -wālī), One who sells fish, fishmonger

def: s. f. Stubbornness.
s. f. Pertness, perverseness, obstinacy.

ety: Prk. मच्छडिआ; S. मत्स्य+र or ल+इका
def: s.f. A fish; — the sign Pisces; — the fish-insect; — an ear-ring made in the form of a fish; — a muscle or tendon (of the arm or leg, — e.g. tumhārī to ḵẖūb maćhliyāṅ khaṛī-huʼī haiṅ)
more: n. f. S. मत्स्यी; Pr. machchho, māch . 1. A fish. 1. Tāzī machhlī daryāo kī! Street cry. Fresh river fish! ho! 2. Machhlī to nahīṅ ki saṛ jāëgī . Prov. Saying. It's not fish that it will rot. (It will keep). 3. Machhlī bechan baiṭhī machhvainī, līye tarājū, chhūrī, Dekh gāhak ko lagī bulāne, 'le maiṅ dūṅgī pūrī! 'dekh tū machhlī merī!' E. Play. The fish wife to sell fish sits down with scales and knife both by, A customer espied she cries 'Full weight I'll give, come buy!' 'Behold, what rare fish I supply!' 4. Torā gāṅveṅ machharī bikāt bā, su-lachhnī, kāhe ke rindalis dāl? Torā chhātī par duī lāl baratbā, kāhe misavalisī gāl . Bhoj. Song. Birha. In your own village fish is sold, why, fair one, cook you dāl? Two rubies on your breasts bright glow, why rub your cheeks at all? 5. def: A Hin. metre. It has four lines each of 10 + 8 + 8 + 6 + 8 = 40 instants. The final syllable of each line must be long; but the last three syllables must not form a ragan (-⏑-). The first two and the last two charans rhyme. Subordinate rhymes occur at the cæsural pause. Kellogg .

ety: n. of place fr. دار (for دور
def: s.m. Place of turning or returning; axis; pivot; centre; — a place within which anything revolves, an orbit; a circumference; — a place where anyone stops or stands, station, seat; that on which anything stands or rests, or depends; ground (of), basis; — dependence; — the point upon which a question (or the like) turns; — (for madāruʼl-mahāmm), Name of a celebrated Muḥammadan saint (held in great reverence by Bhaṭiyāras and Kuṅwār Musalmāns; his tomb is at Makanpur, Kānhpur); the month jumāda-ʼl-awwal (so called after the saint Madār)
more: n. f. S. मन्दार A plant; Asclepias gigantea , Wat. See āk .
n. m. دور going round. 1. Orbit. 2. Basis. 3. Dependance. Ab to sārī bātoṅ kā tumhīṅ par madār hai . 4. A saint worshipped by Mahomedans. madār-i-arzī . The earth's orbit.

(noun of place from دور Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. 1. A circumference, a centre, a place of turning or returning. 2. A place where one stops or stands, a station, a seat. عزت مدار iﻌzzat-madār, The seat of glory. مدار المـﮩام madāru-l-mahāmm, The minister, i. e. the centre of affairs. مدار کار madār-i-kār, A chief manager of business. 3. Name of a celebrated Musalmān saint.
s. f. Name of a plant. (Asclepias gigantea).
subs: 'The centre of affairs,' prime-minister; regent; chief manager of business

ety: Prk. मंदालं; S. मन्दारं
def: s.f. The plant Asclepias gigantea (syn. āk), &c. (see mandār).
s.m. An elephant; — a hog; — a cheat, rogue.

ety: S. मदार+इन्+कः
def: s.m. A conjurer, juggler; a tumbler, merry-andrew (esp. the master or director of the fun); a bear-dancer; monkey-man.
more: n. m. 1. The followers of Madār . 2. A bear-dancer; juggler. Nāche kūde bāṅdrā, māl madārī khāë . Prov. The monkey does the leaps, the showman profit reaps. (One does the work, another has the pay). madāryā huqqah , A huqqah with a long stem.

ety: mādar, q.v.+ī = S. इन्
def: adj. Maternal, motherly
more: adj. Maternal, motherly. ¶ (dakh.) See نمونه namūna.
s. (dakh.) Example, model, pattern: see نمونه namūna.

ety: A. madār + Prk. इओ = S. इकः
def: adj. & s.m. Of or relating to the saint Madār; — a follower of Madār
subs: s.m. A ḥuqqa, with a long stem.

def: s.m. pl. (of madraj, fr. درج; see darja), Steps, stages; degrees; ascents; ways.
more: n. m. pl. of درجه The steps or stages of a business.
s. plur. (of درجه or مدرج from درج Heb. and Aram. ) Steps, degrees, ascents, ways.

def: s.m. pl. (of madrasa, q.v.), Colleges; schools.

ety: A. madār + Prk. इओ = S. इकः
def: adj. & s.m. Of or relating to the saint Madār; — a follower of Madār
more: s. m. The followers of Madār. See مدار sign. 3.
subs: s.m. A ḥuqqa, with a long stem.

def: n. m. 1. A prime minister; regent. 2. A principal manager of affairs. madār-i-dāvā . Ground of claim or action. madār-i-sāat ; S. अहोरात्रवृत्त; H. ghaṛī-chakkar . An hour circle. madār-i-qamrī . The moon's orbit.

ety: for madad, fr. S. मद+दा
def: s.f. (dialec.) An intoxicating preparation of opium, betel-leaves, &c. (for smoking; syn. madad, madak).

ety: S. मर्दितं
def: s.m. (dialec.) A gall, or sore (on the shoulder of a horse, &c.).
s.f. (Dakh.) A female go-between employed in arranging a marriage.
more: s. f. (dakh.) A female gobetween employed in looking out for a wife.

ety: inf. n. of مدّ 'to extend, stretch,' &c.
def: s.f. Extension, lengthening, production; prolonging, prolongation; protraction; delaying; — swelling (of a river); flux (of the sea); flood-tide; the mark (ٓ) placed sometimes over alif (thus آ), which gives it a long broad sound; — a similar mark drawn over a word to denote the commencement of a fresh paragraph or sentence, or over different entries (or heads of entries) in an account, or to indicate the insertion of a new item; (hence) entry; head; article; column; — a peculiar mode of writing certain words in accounts
more: n. f. Lengthening; extension; prolongation; protraction. 2. The mark (˜) placed over alif , thus اَ, when the letter is pronounced long and broad. 3. Article; column ( khānah 4, 6). 4. A sign denoting the commencement of a paragraph or the insertion of a new item. 5. A head of accounts. 6. A peculiar mode of writing certain words in accounts. 7. Flood tide. madd-i-amānat . Head of deposits. madd-i-muqābil. Per contra. mad meṅ ānā , v. n. To come under a heading. madd-i-nazar, madd-i-nigāh , n. m. 1. (H. nazar-gherā ) The limit of one's vision; the horizon. 2. The object of regard or affection. 3. Object (اراده 2, 4). madd-i-nazar , adv. Before one's eyes; in view; in sight. is mad meṅ . G. G. Under this head. mad'de ; Pop. maddhe , adv. On account of ( bābat ).

(Heb. and Aram. ) s. f. 1. Extension, production, lengthening. 2. The flux of the sea, flood-tide. مد وجزر madd-o-jazr, Tide, the flux and reflux of the sea. 3. The mark (~) placed sometimes over alif, thus آ, when the letter is pronounced long and broad. 4. A peculiar mode of writing certain words in accounts. مد ظله madda z̤illu-hu, May his shadow, or power be extended. مد نظر madd-i-naz̤ar, or مد نگاه madd-i-nigāh, s. f. 1. Extension or stretch of sight, as far as the eye can reach, the object of sight, regard, prospect, before the eyes, in view, in sight. 2. Kind looks, encouragement. ¶ mudd, A dry measure equal to one and one third rat̤l, or according to others to two rat̤l.
subs: To come under the head (of, kī)

or madd-ě-nigāh, s.f. & m. Extension or stretch of sight, limit of vision; the horizon; object of sight, or regard, or affection; prospect; object; — kind looks, encouragement; — adv. Before the eyes, in view, in sight, in prospect
Flux and reflux (of the sea), tide

ety: for A. مادة māddat, fem.; v.n. fr. مدّ 'to extend,' &c.
def: s.m. Matter, material; source, root; derivation; — faculty, capacity; — a matter, affair, subject, article, clause.
more: (from مدّ) s. m. A matter, subject, article, affair, clause.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَادَه (māda). Doublet of مَاتَا (mātā).

ety: for A. مدة maddat, n. of un. fr. madd, q.v.
def: s.m. A lengthening, an extension, a prolongation; the mark (ٓ) placed sometimes over alif, when the letter is pronounced long and broad ( = madd); — a long vowel (as ا َ ā, or و ُ ū or ی ِ, i.e. ا, و, and ی, when quiescent, and preceded by the short vowels fatḥa, ẓamma, and kasra respectively)
more: n. m. The long vowels وا and ي when preceded by a letter which has their homogeneous short vowel.
adj. H. mūṅḍ head. Principal; chief; head. muḍh , n. m. 1. A chief or headman ( sardār , 1—4). 2. A ringleader.

s. m. (extension) The long vowels, or و,ا and ي, when quiescent, and preceded by a letter which has their homogeneous short vowel; i. e. ا preceded by fatʼḥa, و by ẓamm and ي by kasr. See مد madd, signif. 3.

ety: intens. n. fr. madḥ, q.v.
def: s.m. One who praises much, encomiast, panegyrist.
more: (adj. superl. of مدح) s. m. An encomiast, praiser, panegyrist.

ety: fr. maddāḥ
def: s.f. Praising, extolling; encomium, panegyric.
s.f. pl. (of madd, q.v.), Heads; entries; columns.
in view, bear in mind
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مَدِّ نَظَر (maddi naẓar).

ety: S. मध्यः, मध्यं, and मध्ये
def: adj. Middle, midmost, central; intermediate, intervening; average; mean, &c.; — s.m. Middle; centre; waist; — mean; — temperament; — adv. In the middle (of, -ke), amidst, amid (see madhya).

ety: S मध्यमः
def: adj. Middle, middlemost; intermediate, &c. ( = madh, q.v.); middling; medium; mean; temperate; fair, passable; — inferior; ordinary; common; — dull, faint, faded (as colour, &c.); slack, slow, sluggish (see madhyam); — s.m. Gold (or silver) of medium quality; — (in Music) the fourth or middle note of the gamut ( = maddham sur)
more: adj. S. मध्यम 1. See dhīmā . 2. Fair; middling; passable, Is mahīne kā phal maddham hai . 3. Low; inferior; common. Yeh maddham, maiṅ jāt kā uttam . 4. Dull; faint; faded. Raṅg maddham paṛ gayā . 5. Sluggish; slack; slow. Bāzār maddham hai. maddham, maddham sur , n. m. ( Mus .) The fourth or middle note of the gamut. maddham purush , ( Gram ). The 2nd person or person spoken to. maddham k. , v. a. To mitigate; moderate; modify; temper; reduce. maddham, yā narm bechnā , v. n. 1. To sell at a moderate price. 2. To undersell.
subs: v.t. To sell at a moderate or a reduced price; — to undersell
v.t. To moderate, slacken, abate, reduce, mitigate, modify; to temper, &c.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀫𑀤𑁆𑀥𑀫 (maddhama), from Sanskrit मध्यम (madhyama).

def: adj. corr. of madhya-stha, q.v.s.v. madhya.

ety: Prk. मड्डिआ; S. मर्दिता
def: s.f. Dregs, sediment, grounds, lees, feculency; — (dialec.) adj. & s.m. Stupid, doltish; — blockhead, booby, simpleton, fool.
more: (dakh.) 1. s. Dregs, sediment: see درد durd. 2. adj. Lazy, slothful, idle. ( Harris ).

ety: rel. n. fr. mādda, q.v.
def: adj. Material; — natural, constitutional; — s.m. The material world (opp. to mujarrad).

ety: maḍḍī, q.v.+panā = Prk. प्पणअं = S. त्व (Vedic त्वन)+कं
def: s.m. Stupidity, foolishness, &c.

ety: for A. مادية, abst. fr. māddī
def: s.f. Materiality, substantialness.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَادِّیَت (māddiyat), from Arabic مَادِّيَّة (māddiyya). By surface analysis, مَادَّہ (mādda) + ـِیَت (-iyat)

def: s.f. pl. (of māddīyat), Material beings or things.
s.m. (dialec.) Hump (of a bull, &c.; cf. mandū).
more: n. m. S. ककुद The hump of the Indian bull. Āṅkh gaḍḍū, nāk maddū, sohnī nāoṅ! Rus. Prov. Hollow eyes and a nose like a hump, and a pretty name forsooth!

def: s.f. corr. of مدرا madirā, q.v. s.f. Spirituous liquor; wine; any inebriating drink; — nectar; — a wagtail; — a species of Sanskrit metre.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit मदिरा (madirā).

ety: n. of place fr. دفن 'to bury'
def: s.m. Burial-place, tomb.
more: (noun of place from دفن) s. m. A tomb, a burying-place.

ety: pass. part. of دفن 'to bury'
def: part. Buried, interred; concealed, hid (treasure under ground); — underground; — filled up, fallen in (a well).
more: adj. دفن interred. 1. Buried; interred; underground. 2. Cncealed; hidden.
(part. pass. of دفن) adj. Buried, concealed, interred.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَدْفُون (madfūn), from Arabic مَدْفُون (madfūn).

def: Madagascar (an island and country off the east coast of Africa)
wety: Borrowed from English Madagascar.

r: madgur, madgurasī, Macropteronatus magur, Silarus pelorius
def: s.m. = S مدگرسي मद्गुरसी madgurasī, s.f. A species of sheat-fish, Macropteronatus magur, or Silarus pelorius.

r: madgurasī, madgur, Macropteronatus magur, Silarus pelorius
def: s.f. = S مدگر मद्गुर madgur, s.m. A species of sheat-fish, Macropteronatus magur, or Silarus pelorius.

ety: S. मधुः
def: s.m. Honey; the juice or nectar of flowers (see madhu); — the grape; — wine; ardent spirit; intoxicating liquor or drug; — intoxication ( = mad, q.v.); pride, arrogance; — passion, lust, lasciviousness; — the season of spring; — prime or bloom (of youth); — warmly-mellowing charms
subs: or madh-meṅ ānā, To attain to puberty
adj. = mad-mātā, q.v.s.v. mad, and madhu-mātā, q.v.s.v. madhu.

def: n. m. S. मद्या Pr. majjā . 1. Intoxication. 2. ( madhrā ) Wine; spirits; intoxicating drugs. Āp hī mad ko pān kare, āp hī hot achet, Tulsī! bidh prakār ko dukh utpat yeh het . Tulsī Dās. Himself he drinks, himself he makes intoxicate, Tulsī! thus for himself doth man e'er pains create. 3. Pride; arrogance. 4. The prime or bloom of youth. 5. Warmly-mellowing charms; ripeness' gushing juices; the grape. Mad ṭapke vākī chhatiyan se . Ripeness' gushing juices trickle from her breasts. 6. The sexual passion; lasciviousness. madh-pān k. , v. a. To drink wine. mad par ānā, madh meṅ ānā , v. n. To attain puberty. Ek purakh jab mad par āve lākhoṅ nārī saṅg lipṭāve, Jab woh nārī mad par āve tab voh nārī nar kahlāve . Riddle. Ans. Ām mango. A male when he's with passion warm, Linked with him maids in thousands swarm, But when the maids full ripe are grown By male names then they 're only known. ( Ambyā , the young mango is feminine; ām , the full-sized mango, is masculine). madh-mātī, mad-mātā , adj. 1. Drunk; intoxicated. 2. Proud; haughty. 3. Youthful; blooming. 4. Lustful; wanton; lascivious. madha'ī , n. f. A drunkard. Charsī kī z ṭal, aur bhaṅgaṛ kī bhabkār, Afīmī kī ṭūl, madhaī kā khumār . The hemp-drunk's wild romance, the bhang-imbiber's twaddle, The opium-eater's swing, the drunkard's sick-head muddle.
more: adj. S. मध्य 1. Middle. Nā kuchh ādi, aṅt kuchh nāhīṅ, nā kuchh maddh judūsā, Sumran, bhajan kare ko kā ko, ye to khyālaī aisā . Kabīr. Beginning nor yet end is His, nor mediate can be thought, To whom your beads and worship then? vain fancies misbegot. 2. Average; intermediate. madh mol . Average price.
s. m. 1. Ardent spirit. 2. The nectar or honey of flowers. 3. Honey. 4. The season of spring. 5. The month Chait (March-April). 6. Name of a demon, slain by Kr̤ish̤ṇa.
1. adv. Amidst, amid; centre, middle. 2. s. m. Temperament.

ety: inf. n. of مدح 'to praise'
def: s.f. Praising; praise, eulogium, encomium, commendation; applause; approbation; recommendation
more: n. f. Eulogy; encomium (تعريف). madah-khuān , n. m. An eulogist; encomiast; a panegyrist. madah o zam . Praise and censure.
s. f. Praise, eulogium, encomium, commendation. مدح خوان madḥ-ḵẖẉān (singing praise) An eulogist, a bard, encomiast.
subs: s.m. Eulogist, encomiast, panegyrist; a bard

def: part. = مڙها maṛhā (p.p. of maṛhnā), q.v.
s.m. corr. of mānḍhā, q.v.

ety: Prk. मंडवओ, मंडअओ; S. मण्डप+कः; — or Prk. मढओ; S. मठ+कः
def: s.m. A temporary building, or an open hall or shed, or a shamiyāna (adorned with flowers and leaves) erected on festive occasions (as at marriages, &c.).
more: 1. (from मंडप ) s. m. A temporary building, an open shed or hall, adorned with flowers and erected on festive occasions, as at marriages, &c. 2. (from مڙهنا) adj. Lined, covered or topped (as, a drum with parchment).

ety: Prk. मंडविआ; S. मण्डपिका; — or i.q. maṛhī, q.v.
def: s.f. A small shed, a hut, cottage.
more: s. f. A cottage, a hut.

r: mādhava, mādhav, mādho, mādhau, madhu, lit.
ety: fr. madhu; lit. 'made of honey, sweet; relating to spring, vernal'
def: s.m. An epithet of Krishṇ (Vishṇu), and of Paraśu-ram (as an incarnation of Vishṇu), and of a celebrated Hindū scholar, son of Māyaṇ; a proper name; — an epithet of the month Baisākh (April-May); spring; — (S. mādhavam) sweetness
more: s. m. A name of Kr̤ish̤ṇa or Vish̤ṇu.
subs: s.f. corr. of mādhavī-latā, q.v.s.v. mādhavī.

def: s.f. Honey-sugar; clayed or candied sugar; — an intoxicating drink made of honey, &c. ( = mādhvīk); — ʻspring-flower,' a large creeper bearing white fragrant flowers, Gaertnera racemosa; — a procuress, bawd; — an epithet of Durgā
subs: vulg. mādho-latā, s.f. The creeper Gaertnera racemosa ( = mādhavī).

def: adj. Intoxicated, drunk.
s. f. A large creeper bearing white fragrant flowers. (Gærtnera racemosa).
adj. دﮩش was confounded. 1. ( matvālā ) Intoxicated. 2. Senseless; insensible; stupefied.
n. f. 1. Intoxication. 2. Insensibility.

s.m. Honey; the juice or nectar of flowers; anything sweet; mead; sugar; liquorice; — sweetness; — a spirituous liquor obtained from the blossoms of the Bassia latifolia; any sweet intoxicating drink; wine; spirituous liquor; — water; — the season of spring; the month Ćait (March-April); — the tree Bassia latifolia; — name of an Asur or Daitya slain by Vishṇu; — name of a metre in Sanskrit
more: n. m. 1. Honey. 2. The honey or nectar of flowers. 3. A spirituous liquor from the blossoms of the Bassia latifolia . 4. The season of spring; the month Chaitra (March-April). Madhu basant, madhu chaitr, madhu madhu, madrā, makraṅd, Madhu jal, madhu pai, madhu sudhā Madsūdan Gobind . Anek-ārth Kosh. Sweet's spring, sweet's summer, honey, flowers, and wine, Spring-water's sweet, and milk, and Krishan mine, madhu , adj. Sweet; dulcet. madhu-kosh , n. m. The honeycomb or hive. madhu-mākhī ; Sant. dumur rasa , n. f. The honey bee ( shahd kī makkhī ).
subs: vulg. madhup, s.m. 'The honey-drinker,' a bee
s.m., or madhu-purī, s.f. Names of the city of Mathurā
s.m. An offering of honey and milk (or honey, butter, and curds) to an honoured guest, or to a Brāhman at a sacrifice, or to the bridegroom on his arrival at the door of the father of the bride
s.m. The middlemost (the 51st) of Viśvāmitra's 101 sons
s.m. pl. All the (101) sons of Viśvāmitra
s.m. 'Destroyer of the demon Madhu,' an epithet of Vishṇu or Krishṇa

s.m. (f. -karī), 'Honey-maker,' a bee; — a lover, a gay Lothario, a libertine; — the plants Eclipta prostrata, Asparagus racemosa, Achyranthes aspera; the sweet lime; — a name of Uddhav (a relation of Krishṇa's)
s.m. Honey-comb; bee-hive; — scrotum (of a goat)
s.m. Name of a demon slain by Vishṇu
s.m. Name of a rāginī or musical mode
adj. (f. -ī), Intoxicated, drunk; — intoxicated or excited by the spring, &c. (see madh-mātā, and mad-mātā)
s.m. Spring month, the month Ćait (March-April)
adj. (f. -ī), Consisting of (or abounding in) honey, made of honey; honeyed; sweet as honey; sweet, luscious
vulg. madhu-ban, s.m. 'The forest of the demon Madhu,' on the Jamnā (where Śatru-ghna, after slaying Lavaṇa, son of Madhu, founded the city of Madhurā or Mathurā)
or madhu-han, s.m. A destroyer of honey; — a collector of honey; — name of a particular bird of prey; — ʻslayer of the demon Madhu,' an epithet of Krishṇa; — a soothsayer.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit मधु (madhu). Doublet of مَے (mai, “liquor, wine”).

def: s. m. A name of Mathurā, q. v.

def: s. m. 1. A bee. See الي alī. 2. n. prop. Name of Uddhav, a relation of Kr̤ish̤ṇa.
s.f. = madhūkrī, q.v.

ety: S. माधुकरी, fr. मधु+कर
def: s.f. A kind of cake or bread baked on live coals (used among faqīrs); — food given as alms to pilgrims.
more: s. f. 1. A kind of bread baked on live coals (used among the faḳīrs ). 2. Victuals given in alms to pilgrims.

ety: S. माधुकरी, fr. मधु+कर
def: s.f. A kind of cake or bread baked on live coals (used among faqīrs); — food given as alms to pilgrims.
s.m. Intoxicating drink, spirituous liquor.

ety: S. मधूलकः, and मधूलकं
def: s.m. A species of Bassia which grows in watery places; — sweetness; honey.
s. f. A honey bee.
s. m. The month Chait (March-April).
s. m. Name of a rāginī or musical mode.

(part. pass. of فعل Heb. and Aram. ) 1. Accusative or other objective case. 2. The participle passive.

ety: A. مفعولية, fr. mafʻūlī, rel. n. fr. mafʻūl
def: s.f. The condition of the object or accusative, accusativeness; — the state of being passive, the condition of the passive voice, passiveness, passivity.

def: s.m. A road, &c. ( = mārg, and māṅg, qq.v.). s.f. Death
subs: or marg-ě-mufājāt, or marg-ě-nāgahānī, Sudden death

def: s.m. contrac. of magadh, q.v.

ety: Prk. मग्गो; S. मार्गः
def: s.m. Road, path, way (cf. māṅg; — syn. bāṭ; rāh)
more: s. m. A road. مگ ديکهنا mag dekhnā, To expect, to wait for.
subs: (-kā) = bāṭ dekhnā, and rāh dekhnā, q.v.

def: s.m. contrac. of māgh, q.v.

adj. & s.m. Relating or belonging to the province of Magadh; — a native or an inhabitant of Magadh; — name of a mixed caste (still numerous in Gujarāt, and called Bhāṭs; they are said to have sprung from a Vaiśya father and Kshatriya mother); an individual of that caste; the poetical panegyrist of a king, a bard, minstrel, rhapsodist (whose duty it is to recite the praises of sovereigns, their genealogy, and the deeds of their ancestors; and to attend on the march of an army, and animate the soldiers by martial songs; — syn. bhāṭ, karkhait).
s.m. The country of South Bihār (including Patna, Gayā, &c.); — an inhabitant of that country; — a family bard or minstrel (who sings the praises of a chief's ancestry in his presence)
more: s. m. Name of a country including Paṭnā, Gayā, &c.
subs: (S. māgadhī-bhāshā), s.f. The Magadh language, the dialect of Bihār; — name of a Prākrit dialect (used in Siam, Burmah, and Ceylon, where it is called Pālī)
s.m. The country of South Bihār (i.q. Magadh)
(˚dha+īś˚), s.m. King of the Magadhas; — a name of Paran-tap, and of Jarāsandh (one of the adversaries of Krishṇ).

def: n. m. 1. A native of Magadh or Behār. 2. A minstrel or rhapsodist ( bhāṭ , 7, and kaṛkait ).
more: s. m. A bard, a minstrel, whose duty it is to recite the praises of sovereigns, their genealogy and the deeds of their ancestors, in their presence; and to attend on the march of an army, and animate the soldiers by martial songs. The minstrels form a particular cast, said to spring from a Vaisya father and Ksh̤atriya mother: in mythology, they are said to have been created at once by the will of Siva. Under the name of Bhāṭs, they are still numerous in some parts of India, especially Gujarāt, where they are a privileged tribe.

def: n. m. The tract of country comprising Paṭna, Gaya, etc. Magghā meṅ marnā, agle janam meṅ gadhā bannā . Prov. In Maggha who from earth doth pass in his next life is born as ass. magadh bhāshā , n. m. A Prakrit dialect, much used in Siam, Barma, and Ceylon where it is called Pālī: the dialect of Behar, and esp. of Paṭna and Gayā. magdhī, maghai'yā , n. m. 1. A native or resident of Maggha. 2. A tribe of agriculturists in Behar. 3. A caste of Brahmans.

ety: S. मागधाः
def: s.m. pl. The inhabitants or people of the province of Māgadh.

s.f. The language of the Magadhas (being one of the principal forms of Prākrit, and nearly the same as that used in the sacred books of the Buddhists and Jains); — a kind of jasmine, Jasminum auriculatum; — long pepper; — white cumin; anise or dill; a species of cardamoms (grown in Gujarāt); — refined sugar.
